skill.name: sragent
skill.description: Query the Sequence Read Archive, GEO, and related publication sources with SRAgent. The skill helps convert genomics accessions, inspect sequencing metadata, identify single-cell technologies, and retrieve linked papers.
skill.summary: Use SRAgent to convert SRA and GEO accessions, inspect genomics metadata, and retrieve linked publications.
skill.author: ArcInstitute

security_audit.summary: Static findings are mostly expected for a research workflow skill that runs the SRAgent CLI, queries public genomics services, and downloads papers. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found, but troubleshooting guidance can expose API keys or .env contents and should be treated as a publication warning.

medium_findings.title: Troubleshooting Guidance Can Reveal Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill includes commands that echo API key variables, grep environment variables containing API_KEY, and print .env contents. This is useful for setup debugging, but it can disclose secrets into chat logs or terminal output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show secret-related environment checks and .env printing. I found disclosure risk, but no evidence that the skill sends those secrets to an external endpoint.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution With User-Supplied Research Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run SRAgent through shell commands and bash_tool examples. This is central to the skill, but accession values and CSV paths should be validated before being placed in shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell execution guidance is explicit and repeated. The examples are legitimate, so the risk depends on whether the host agent validates user-provided accessions and paths.

medium_findings.title: Network Downloads and Local File Writes Are Expected Behavior
medium_findings.description: The papers workflow retrieves publications from public sources and writes PDFs or enriched CSV files to output directories. This creates normal network and filesystem exposure for a publication-retrieval skill, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file and network behavior is documented as paper retrieval and metadata lookup. I found no destination suggesting unauthorized data transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive-File and Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: BioSample accession examples such as SAMN are biological identifiers, not Windows SAM database access. Old instrument names such as LS454 are sequencing platforms, not weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text clearly defines genomics accession and sequencing platform references. These matches do not represent operating system secrets or cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Access Is Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The ~/.claude/skills path is used to install the skill for Claude Code. It is not evidence of stealthy hidden-file access or unauthorized reading of user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path appears inside documented installation and troubleshooting steps. No hidden file content is read at these locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Invocation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on shell commands to invoke SRAgent and related setup tools. This should run only with user-approved inputs and normal command execution safeguards.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present and intended. It is potentially dangerous only if an agent passes untrusted values into commands without validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret-Printing Troubleshooting Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The references include commands that can display environment variables and .env contents. Marketplace users should avoid printing secret values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and directly related to credentials. The risk is disclosure through logs, not confirmed external exfiltration.

content.user_title: Analyze SRA and GEO Genomics Metadata
content.value_statement: Researchers often need to connect SRA, GEO, BioProject, and publication records before analysis. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through SRAgent commands for accession conversion, metadata inspection, and paper retrieval.
actual_capabilities: Converts GEO, SRA, BioProject, experiment, and run accession formats using SRAgent command examples.
Guides metadata extraction for organism, platform, library layout, tissue, and single-cell technology fields.
Supports paper lookup and open-access PDF download workflows linked to SRA accessions.
Provides batch workflows for CSV files containing multiple SRA or GEO accessions.
Documents BigQuery-backed analysis paths and Entrez-only fallback behavior.
Includes reference tables for common accession patterns and sequencing metadata fields.
limitations: Requires a separate SRAgent installation before commands can run.
Needs API keys or credentials for some model, NCBI, CORE, and BigQuery features.
Paper downloads depend on open-access availability and source rate limits.
Metadata completeness depends on public database records and may vary by accession.

use_cases.title: Convert accession identifiers
use_cases.description: Convert a GEO series or BioProject accession into SRA experiment and run accessions for downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Audit sequencing metadata
use_cases.description: Check whether public experiments match required organism, platform, paired-end, or single-cell criteria.

use_cases.title: Collect dataset publications
use_cases.description: Find linked PubMed records, DOIs, and available full-text papers for a set of SRA accessions.

prompt_templates.title: Convert one accession
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for a single dataset identifier.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SRAgent to convert GSE121737 into SRA experiment accessions. Summarize the result in a short table.

prompt_templates.title: Check experiment metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Verify whether one accession matches analysis criteria.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SRAgent to check SRX4967527 for organism, platform, library layout, and whether it is single-cell RNA-seq.

prompt_templates.title: Download linked papers
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieve publications for a dataset after confirming accessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SRAgent to find publications for PRJNA498286 and download available open-access papers into a dedicated output folder.

prompt_templates.title: Run a batch curation workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Given my CSV of SRA accessions, use SRAgent to add organism, sequencing platform, single-cell status, DOI, and download status columns.

output_examples.output: A concise table listing the GEO series, linked SRA study, and returned SRX experiment accessions, with any missing mappings noted.
A short evidence-based answer covering single-cell status, detected library preparation, sequencing platform, organism, and confidence notes.

output_examples.input: Download papers for a list of SRA experiments.
output_examples.output: A summary of processed accessions, linked PubMed IDs, DOI availability, successful downloads, failed downloads, and saved file locations.

best_practices: Validate accession formats before running shell commands or batch workflows.
Use redacted environment checks instead of printing API key values or .env contents.
Start with Entrez conversion, then run deeper metadata or paper workflows on confirmed accessions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste raw API keys, service account files, or .env contents into the chat.
Do not run paper downloads at high concurrency when source services are rate limited.
Do not assume every public accession has complete metadata or an open-access publication.

faq.question: Does this skill install SRAgent automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup and usage guidance, but SRAgent must be installed separately in the working environment.

faq.question: Which accessions can it work with?
faq.answer: It documents workflows for GEO, SRA, BioProject, BioSample, ENA, experiment, and run accession formats.

faq.question: Can it identify single-cell RNA-seq datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides SRAgent queries that combine metadata, protocol text, publication abstracts, and sequence-file signals.

faq.question: Does paper retrieval always download PDFs?
faq.answer: No. Downloads depend on linked publications, DOI discovery, open-access availability, network access, and source limits.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: Some features require model provider keys. NCBI, CORE, and Google Cloud credentials improve rate limits or enable optional services.

faq.question: What security precautions are recommended?
faq.answer: Avoid printing secrets, validate user-provided paths and accessions, and review downloads before using them in later workflows.
